description: SPEESH Mr LINCOLN AT SPRINGFIELD July 17 185S Mr LINCOLN said Fellow CitizensAnolher election which is deemed an important one is approaching and as I suppose tin Re publican party will without much dithcul ty elect their State ticket But in re gard to the Legislature we the Repub licans labor under some disadvantages In the first place we have a Legisla ture to elect upon an apportionment of the representation made several year ago when the proportion of the popu lation was far greater in the south to the north than it now is and inasmuch as our opponents hold almost entire sway in the south and we a correspondingly large majority m the north the fact that we are now to be represented as we were years ago when the population was different is to us a very great dis advantage IVe had in the year lttoft according to law a census or an enu meration of the inhabitants taken for the purpose of a new apportionment of representation We know what a fair apportionment of representation upon that census would gve us We know givefnelld1Uitir1nrjlYy Ift liV1 Hi ten more members of the Legislature than they con
